questionHtml: Using the graph of y=8x+x44-x3, suggest a reason why the approximation given by Euler’s method in part (a) is not a good estimate to the actual value of y at x=1.5.
markschemeHtml:
gradient changes rapidly (during the interval considered)  OR

the curve has a vertical asymptote at x=43 =1.5874…            R1

 

[1 mark]

meta.title: Using the graph of y=8x+x44-x3, suggest a reason why the approximation given by Euler’s method in part (a) is not a good estimate to the actual value of y at x=1.5.
